entry.title: Material Listing & Exclusion data
entry.content:
Recently, for one business requirement, the solution of “Material Exclusion” was finalized with our client.

Wrt the above, the client wanted to know, if there is a table – having the list of all Material/Plants having active exclusions.

The below answers the above:

  • Transaction: SE11
  • Table: KOTG*
  • F4

The tables list is displayed as below

 

 

The above was helpful for me, in creating a Query report.

Hope, the above is useful.

Thank you for your time.

Sumanth Gururaj

entry.title: Public Sector Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
As things continue to heat up with the Public Sector market, we continue to enhance Public Sector Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as well as expand the reach of the solution geographically.   New Central Business Configuration capabilities have allowed us to deliver the public sector specific scope items along with the standard LOB scope items.   With the 2402 release, the initial Global Public Sector Management (PSM) scope bundle becomes available for Canada and Germany.   This scope bundle includes 5 public sector specific scope items, along with the LOB scope items to support core finance functionality.   Plans to expand the Global PSM scope to the full breadth and reach of the US PSM scope are on the roadmap for 2408.   Check out the 2402 release in Process Navigator.

Process Navigator Public Sector US

Process Navigator Public Sector Outside the US

 

The five public sector specific scope items include the unique functionality required for the administration of public and grant funds. 

In addition to the exciting news of the global rollout, other innovations and refinements continue to enhance the solution including:

  • Management of expected revenues via earmarked funds
  • Ability to manually create original budget documents
  • Improvements in Grant Overview and Budget Consumption dashboards
  • Ability to create billing plans for grants
  • Monitor Grants – overview page for grants management
  • Enhanced integration with Concur


Manage Earmarked Funds – Forecast of Revenue

Public Sector entities require the ability to effectively manage and consume approved budgets.  Allowable expenditures may be contingent on the receipt of certain revenues.  Previously released Earmarked Funds document types include Funds Reservations, Funds Pre-commitments, and Funds Commitments.   These Earmarked Funds documents allow for the reservation of expenditure budget in advance of expected procurement activity or direct expenses.   Now delivered is a fourth document type – Forecast of Revenue. 


Value Proposition

  • Visibility to and management of expected revenues
  • Improved cash flow planning
  • Support for strategic decision making.  
  • Ability to smoothly integrate recording of predicted revenues and actual revenue

Capabilities 

  • Create, copy and edit Forecast of Revenue documents
  • Reference and reduce by customer invoices
  • Increase or liquidate as needed
  • Manage attachments
  • Track changes
  • Flexible workflow approvals


Manage Budget Documents – Original Budget
Budget management supports budgeting-based management of funds at public sector institutions where revenues and expenditures are budgeted according to specific criteria, such as area of responsibility, program/function and source of funds. Public sector expenditures are authorized by an approved budget.  Financial transactions are validated against these criteria to ensure compliance with the distributed budget within a state or local authority.  Effective budget management is central and critical to public sector financial management. 

Original budget is used to represent the approved budget in the system.   The ability to upload original budget was previously delivered.   Now original budget documents can be manually created as well.


Value Proposition

  • Improve the ability to manage the approved budget throughout the fiscal year
  • Increase flexibility in the budget process

Capabilities

  • Create, copy and edit original budget documents
  • Manage text and attachments
  • Flexible workflow approvals
  • Immediate control of expenses and analytics visibility

 

Improvements in Grant Overview and Budget Consumption dashboards

Embedded analytics are delivered with extensive KPIs to support effective grant and budget management.   The respective SAC stories contain multiple pages of comprehensive data with the ability to filter and sort as business needs require.  These analytics allow specialists and managers to have immediate access to accurate information and trends. Continued enhancements to the dashboards reflect expanded flexibility and features available in the grants and budget management.

Value Proposition

  • Allows overview of grants by user status
  • Provides grant history that considers user statuses
  • Enables a budget situation based on workflow status
  • Delivers real-time information about budget allocation and consumption

Capabilities

  • Grants user status capabilities are now visualized on the Status page in the Grants Overview app
  • Principle of Prudence is now available on the Budget Consumption Dashboard by adding a new filter for Budget page
  • Ability to filter the budget data based on the workflow status is now available on the Budget page for the Budget Consumption Dashboard

 

Ability to create billing plans for grants
One of the most important business processes within Grants Management is the collection of revenues from the granting sponsors. Most grants are managed on a reimbursement basis.   As expenses are incurred, they are appropriately billed to the sponsors.  For this purpose the system supports manual billing and the following automated billing processes: resource related billing based on expenses, resource related billing based on payments, billing plans and periodic billing.

Value Proposition

  • Simplify the grants management billing process
  • Automatically create billing documents for the sponsor using flexible rules to determine billing dates and billing amounts

Capabilities 

  • New Manage Grants Billing Plan app to enable customers to maintain billing plans for grants
  • Ability to freely define billing items by entering flexible billing dates and billing amounts
  • Alternative option to generate periodic billing plans by entering periodicity, billing date rule, and billing amount (for example, billing US$100 every month on the last day of the month for six months)
  • Ability to copy billing items and enter billing dates and billing amounts.
  • After entering the grant, the total billing amount with a Valid From Date and a Valid To Date is copied from the grant into the billing plan.

 

 

Monitor Grants – grants management overview page

Use the Monitor Grants Fiori app to visualize key grant monitoring data that can enable the organization to perform efficient and reliable analysis of the grants.

Value Proposition

When you access the application, by default the dashboard displays the visualizations of all the grants. The page also offers a filter tab with the Grant, Grant Type, Status, and Sponsor filters that you can use to filter and view the visualizations on the dashboard, based on the filter criteria.

Capabilities
The following cards are delivered using the Monitor Grants Fiori app:

  • Grant Billing Details
  • Grants in Approval by Status
  • Grants by Grant Type
  • Grants by Status
  • Grant Revenue
  • Top Sponsors
  • Budget Consumption by Grant

Individual cards can also be added to the user’s home page.

 

Enhanced Integration with Concur

Travel expenses can be responsible for a significant part of the budget consumption. As such SAP Concur integrates seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition for Public Sector to control those travel expenses.  This means that planned or realized expenses check budget and are visible in PSM Commitment and Actual reporting. The budget control checks and updates are triggered by Concur document workflow.

 Value Proposition

  • Enable straightforward integration of travel expenses established in SAP Concur solutions with budgetary and grant accounting processes
  • Make travel-expense accounting compliant with budgetary, fund, and grant accounting regulations
  • Avoid budget overruns through early reservation of budgets
  • Support smooth integration of recording of commitments and actuals

Capabilities 

  • Introduction of an additional value type for expense report obligation in funds and grants management commitment, updating prior to actual posting of expense report
  • Optional reservation of budgets for expected expenditures for travel requests or expense report obligation
  • Automatic reduction of the predecessor commitment by the successor document
  • Active budgetary availability control for commitments and actual expenses
  • Updates to the budgetary ledger


New S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition for Public Sector Learning Journeys
There are three new learning journeys published at the end of October 2023 on Learning.SAP.com, with a new industry landing page for Public Sector coming soon.   These learning journeys offer over 400 pages of educational content and include digital content that will be continuously expanded.  Don’t miss out on an opportunity to develop a deeper understanding of the solution.  The three comprehensive courses include Budget Management, Grants Management, and Fund Accounting. Public Sector Learning Journeys

Budget Management

  • Intro to S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, for Public Sector
  • Master Data
  • Budgeting
  • Budget Consumption and AVC
  • Integrated Components
  • Year End Processing
  • Reporting

Grants Management

  • Introduction
  • Master Data
  • Budgeting
  • Transactions
  • Reporting

Fund Accounting

  • Introduction
  • Advanced Accounting Concepts
  • Integrated Components
  • Reporting

 

As you can see, this release is filled with innovations and enhancements that help public sector run better. While you enjoy the innovations this release brings, check out what else we offer for you below.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself at Mary Ramsrud.

 

📽️ Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

🎯Where to Find More Information:

entry.title: A Quick Guide To ABAP Debugger
entry.content:
While the SAP BTP platform has witnessed significant progress and adoption, offering numerous development tools for SAP platforms, it's noteworthy that SAP GUI for ABAP continues to be widely utilized in many organizations and projects. This document aims to deliver succinct information about the SAP GUI ABAP debugger, specifically catering to SAP functional consultants and ABAP newcomers.

Note that certain features are also accessible during debugging in Eclipse ADT. Personally, I favor using the Eclipse IDE.

Breakpoint: A breakpoint is a specific point in your program where the execution of instructions comes to a halt. In ABAP, there are four ways to interrupt the execution of your program:

  1. Session breakpoint: These breakpoints are specific to the current session of SAP GUI and the user who sets them. Session breakpoints do not function for remote debugging.
  2. External breakpoint: External breakpoints have a duration and apply to the user who sets them, regardless of the session. They are suitable for remote debugging.
  3. Debugger breakpoint: Debugger breakpoints are applicable when a debugger session is active and disappear once the debugger window is closed.
  4. Breakpoint statements: These are specific statements in the ABAP program that pause the program's execution. They can be used in two ways:
    1. BREAK-POINT: Always active for all users.
    2. BREAK <USER_ID>: Triggers only for the specified user.

Note: You cannot set more than 30 breakpoints and 10 watchpoints in a program.

 

To debug a transaction, we can enter /h, /ha, /hs in the command field
  • /h: Turns on the debugger where you can even debug the dialogue programs.
  • /hs: Turns on the system debugger and allows you to debug the system programs, also turns on update debugging.
  • /ha: Turns on the debugging without screen handling. Skips PBO, PAI.

Key commands - The following commands can be used while debugging an ABAP program:

  1. Single step (F5): The single-step command executes one instruction at a time while you go ahead. Suppose you give a single-step command at a subroutine or function call. In that case, the debugger will navigate to the program for that subroutine, a function module, or any other object that is used for the modularization.
  2. Execute (F6): When you take the execute action, the debugger steps over a given line. If the line contains a function or a subroutine the function will be executed, and the result will be returned without debugging each line inside that subroutine or function.
  3. Return (F7): While debugging if you get inside a subroutine or function module with single steps, the return command navigates the debugger to the line where the current function or subroutine was called.
  4. Continue (F8): Executes the whole program and switches to the output screen. (Or halts at the next breakpoint if encountered)

# Ready, set, execute!

Once you put the breakpoint and execute the program, your program will be interrupted during the execution and the ABAP debugger will start in a new window. Below is a picture of the ABAP debugger screen.

The ABAP debugger window depicted above can be divided into six components for better comprehension:

  1. Menu bar
  2. SAP’s standard toolbar
  3. The debugger toolbar
  4. Source code attributes
  5. Tabs and Services of tool
  6. Context menus

Each of the seven components within the ABAP debugger contains multiple options. Let's examine each of them individually.

Menu bar

  1. Debugger menu: The initial menu visible on the menu bar is the Debugger menu. Clicking on it will reveal eleven options. The first four options will be elaborated upon in the subsequent section about the debugger toolbar. Let's now focus on the remaining seven options.
    1. Continue to Cursor (Shift + F8): Continue to cursor option will execute your program till the place where your cursor is blinking in the source code tool of the debugger. If your cursor is above the current line where execution is halted, the "Continue to Cursor" option will just execute the whole program.
    2. Goto statement (Shift + F12): The Goto statement will halt the program execution at the point where your cursor is placed in your source code tool. Unlike Continue to Cursor, the "Goto statement" skips the execution of the statements between your current line and the line where your cursor is present.
    3. Debugger Session: The Debugger session opens a submenu of five more options.
      1. Save: With Save, you can save your debugger layout, including breakpoints, watchpoints, layer-aware configuration, the layout of your debugger subscreens, and the variables you’ve entered into the variable fast display. In the below screenshot, you’ll see the text (Not for START-UP Session). The START-UP session is the default debugger session that is used when launching the ABAP debugger if no user layout is found. When creating a new START-UP session, you cannot specify breakpoints or watchpoints.
      2. Load (Shift + F11): After saving your debugger session, other ABAPers can load the same session by following the menu path Debugger -> Debugger Session -> Load. The debugger load dialog is similar to the debugger save. You can specify whether you want to load the debugger session’s layout, breakpoints, etc.
      3. Overview/Delete: If you click on the Delete option, a new window opens. This window is a transaction in SAP where you can display and delete the saved variants.
      4. Overview/Delete: If you click on the Delete option, a new window opens. This window is a transaction in SAP where you can display and delete the saved variants.
      5. Save layout (Ctrl + Shift + F3): You can rearrange the layout of any of the tabs in the debugger. With the Save layout option, you can save the rearranged tabs. However, SAP will prevent you from saving changes to all but the Desktop tabs. When you save a debugger layout, SAP will remember your custom layouts on all three desktop tabs and they will be available to you every time you launch the ABAP Debugger. We’ll explore more about rearranging the tabs in the later section.
      6. Designation of the User Desktop: With this option, you can rename the Desktop tabs. 
    4. Restart: The restart option restarts the whole program from the beginning.
    5. Exit (Close Application): Closes the whole application and terminates the further execution of the program.
    6. Exit (Application continues running): Closes the Debugger while continuing to run the application.

  2. Edit menu: The Edit menu has five options. Search and Find Next in the edit menu only works in the variables fast display tool. System functions -> Start Garbage Collector option removes the unreferred objects from the memory. Cancel terminates the application without further execution.

  3. Goto menu: If you click on the Goto menu, the first option will be “Goto source code”. Goto source code opens a new ABAP editor window for source code which is being debugged currently. Navigate to option can be used to navigate to different tabs you see on the debugger screen.

  4. Breakpoints: Let’s look at all the options in the Breakpoints menu.

    1. Delete all BPs (Shift + F2): Deletes all the breakpoints including, session breakpoints, and debugger breakpoints. The breakpoint statements and watchpoints, however, will still work.
    2. Deactivate all BPs (Ctrl + Shift + F6): This option deactivates all the breakpoints until the current debugger session is opened.
    3. Activate all BPs (Ctrl + Shift + F7): Activates all the breakpoints if there are any deactivated ones.
    4. Create breakpoint (F9): If you click on “Create breakpoint”, it opens a new dialog box for an option for creating the breakpoints anywhere in the current program. In this dialog box, you can create dynamic breakpoints at different elements of the ABAP program.
      1. ABAP Cmnds: In the ABAP Cmnds tab you can enter a statement of abap program and the dynamic breakpoints will be set at every one of those statements. For example, we can write ‘CALL FUNCTION’ to set the dynamic breakpoints at all the function call statements in the current ABAP program.

         

      2. Method: You can put a dynamic breakpoint on any method that is called in the current session. You can also add a breakpoint at a method call that is specific to the program name or a class.  
      3. Function: A dynamic breakpoint can also be added for a call of a function in the whole session. Notice that there is a column with the header “Free Condition Entry”. In this column, you can add a condition for a conditional breakpoint. Conditional breakpoints are explained later in this document.

         

      4. Form: In the Form tab, you can add a dynamic breakpoint on a call of a subroutine inside the program you want.
      5. Except.: Except. tab can be used to set the dynamic breakpoints on exceptions. Every ABAP exception has a class associated with it. If you don’t know the specific class, then you can put a breakpoint on exception ‘CX_ROOT’, which is the base for all the ABAP exceptions.
      6. Srce Code: The dynamic breakpoints can also be put while debugging at the program source code by specifying the program/include the program name and the row(line).

         

      7. Misc: In this tab, you can ensure that the program stops at the call stack change in the program. By marking the “Unprecise DECFLOAT Computation” checkbox, the execution marker will stop at the point where an imprecise calculation of a floating point number takes place.

         

      8. Message: If you want to find where in the program a message is being called, a breakpoint at the message can help you find that. All you need is the message ID and message number.
    5. Line breakpoint: Select Line breakpoint -> Set/Delete option to put a breakpoint where your cursor currently is. Select Line breakpoint -> Activate/Deactivate to toggle activation or deactivation of the breakpoint of a selected line.
    6. Breakpoint at: The "breakpoint at" opens a context menu of different options to create a dynamic breakpoint. All these options are described in the Create Breakpoint section.
    7. Reload BPs: Reload BPs reload the session and external breakpoints if they’re deactivated.
    8. Save Debugger BPS as: You can save the debugger breakpoints for the current SAP GUI session or current user sessions.
    9. Create Watchpoint: A watchpoint is a special breakpoint that stops the execution of an application whenever the value of a given expression changes, without specifying where it might occur. You can put a watchpoint on a variable or object instance by entering the variable name in the Create watchpoint dialog.

       

    10. Settings: The first option in the settings menu is “System Debugging On/Off”. This enables system debugging of the application. If your system debugging is You can click on, your screen title will show something like below where (/hs) denotes system debugging.

 

SAP’s standard toolbar:

  • Although the standard toolbar of SAP behaves the same for all the applications in SAP, the Save option of ABAP debugger will save the debugger breakpoints as session breakpoints.

 

The debugger toolbar:

  1. The first four icons of the debugger toolbars are the four key comments that are defined at the beginning of this blog.
  2. The “Step Size” icon changes the way a statement is executed. By default, the debugger executes one line at a time, but if you change the step size of the execution, the debugger will execute each operation performed in statements with a chain operator.
  3. The other two icons are for “Create breakpoint” and “Create watchpoint”.
  4. Save layout: This is the icon for the Save Layout option in the debugger menu.

 

Source code attributes:

  1. The part of the debugger screen in the above image shows the attributes of the current program that is being debugged. The first bar has the name of your master program for the current source code and the bar next to it has your current program name. Clicking on the icon opens the source code of the program that you are debugging in a separate window of the ABAP editor.
  2. SY-SUBRC is the return code set by the previously executed ABAP statements. If SY-SUBRC is 0, the previous statement has been executed correctly.
  3. SY-TABIX is a system variable that stores the index of the latest accessed record in an internal table.

Services of tool and Tabs:

The tabs are your work areas in the debugger. You’ll see the debugger tools here. Before we start looking at the tabs, let's get to know the Debugger Tool settings ribbon. This ribbon helps you change how your current tab looks and add or remove different tools like source code, Call Stack, Breakpoints, and so on.

  • Services of the Tool (In the Debugger Tool ribbon) : The Services of the Tool icon launches a popup with additional functionality available in each tool. The functions available differ based on the tool you’re using, but some examples are:
    1. Better filtering of variable lists in the Variable Fast View Tool.
    2. Save Data as an "SE37" variant in the Variable Fast View Tool.
    3. Debug ABAP macros.
  • Some of the debugger tools are explained below:
    1. Source Code: Displays an older view of the source code display.
    2. Call Stack: Shows the call stack of the functions, subroutines, or events. You can backtrack your function call using call stack. You can change the configuration to show only screens in the call stack or only ABAP events in the call stack. By default, both screens and ABAP events are shown.
    3. Variable fast display: You have two Variables tabs for the definition of variables, tables, and objects you’re interested in exploring further. You can save these lists by clicking on the disk icon seen in the screenshot.
      1. The Locals tab shows all variables defined in the current event’s scope. The Globals tab shows all variables that are visible and available in the current event across the ABAP call stack.
      2. If you scroll to the right side you’ll find the absolute type of a variable. You can double-click on the type to open it in a new window.
      3. The Memory Analysis tab can show the memory details of the variable. If shows the bytes of memory allocated to any variable. This might help you to make your code more memory-efficient.
      4. If you change the debugger step size to Statement, the Auto tab will display the return variables of individual statements or method calls chained together.
    4. Breakpoints: You can see the list of breakpoints or watchpoints and perform operations on them which are available in the Breakpoints menu.
    5. Object: The object tool allows you to explore the contents of data variables declared using TYPE REF TO. This typically involves browsing the contents of a class instance when you're dealing with OOP.
    6. Table: Table tool allows you to edit and explore the contents of the internal table at runtime.
    7. Structure: Structure tool displays and allows you to edit the structure in ABAP memory. You can also download structure contents as XML.
    8. Single field: The single field displays the contents of an absolute type. You can edit the field contents as well.
    9. Data Explorer: Allows you to explore and interact with many different kinds of data types, from class instances to tables. This is a very versatile tool if you’re working with different kinds of objects in the debugger.
    10. Memory Explorer: This tool provides you the insights into how much memory your program is using.
    11. Difference tool: This tool can compare similar types of variables for you. This tool also keeps the history of the variables that you’ve compared before.
  • Desktop tabs: The Desktop 1, Desktop 2, and Desktop 3 tabs are yours to play with. All the customizations you do on these desktops can be saved.
  • Standard tab: In the Standard tab where you will work with the Call Stack, Variable fast display, and Source code. You can customize it for the current session.
  • All the other tabs are dedicated tabs for different Services or tools.

 

Context Menus:

  • If you right-click on the source code tool in the ABAP debugger, you will find an option called "ABAP bytecode (debug macro)." Choosing this option transforms your source code from ABAP to ByteCode. When an ABAP program is written and activated, it gets converted into ByteCode, somewhat resembling assembly code. The ABAP runtime interprets this ByteCode. Therefore, while debugging a macro, you can see only the ByteCode rather than the original ABAP source code.
  • To add bookmarks while debugging, right-click on the yellow strip located to the left of the line numbers in the source code. A context menu, similar to the one illustrated below, will appear. This menu allows you to place bookmarks on the clicked line, enabling you to return to that specific point later during the debugging process.

     

 

I've attempted to provide an overview of the key features of the SAP GUI ABAP debugger to the best of my knowledge. As you delve into extensive programs, you may discover and learn to utilize many more features. Happy debugging!

entry.title: SAP Materials Management Introduction
entry.content:
Taking that article, as an introductory step to the module Materials Management "MM". It will be your guiding article to build the base knowledge in that module. It will help you to put yourself on the track of diving deeper vertically in all the topics, that will be listed below.

SAP stands for (Systems, Applications and Products for Enterprise data processing). For SAP MM, It is an ERP system that deals with procuring, processing and production of materials with transactions such as Purchase Order (PO), Purchase Requisition (PR), Contracts, Inventory Valuation, Vendor Management and Materials Requirement Planning. SAP MM is related to the SAP ERP Logistics system. Each system needs data. For SAP MM it needs data related to the materials, vendors, plants, storage locations and inventory management. That data is called Master Data.

Let's have the cycle of SAP MM in a form of a story:

It is starts with a user requesting to purchase materials and services through manual or automated processes in the form of a Purchase Requisition (PR).  After getting the approval on that request from the purchasing department. These PRs are converted to Purchase Orders (PO).

It can be considered as an external purchasing document, that will be sent to the vendor. Vendors are now aware with the material required. Receiving the material in our stock is the process of Goods Receipt (GR). Invoices will be created by the vendor, and the payment will be done. 

Receiving goods from vendors make it subject to be tested for quality. The quality needs to be good, so the goods won't return back to the vendor. Having the quality checked, and everything is good. The goods will be sent to the stock.  

Let's check the organization structure for SAP MM:

In SAP MM, The highest level in the org. structure is the "Client". Inside the client, there can be multiple company codes, which are the legal entities within the org. structure. Inside these company codes, there can be multiple plants. Inside the plants, there can be multiple storage locations, which are used to differentiate between the materials stored. There are also purchasing organizations and purchasing groups within the org. structure. 

Let's discuss the Material Master Data within SAP MM:

Material Master Data contains all the data related to the material characteristics. To create a material master, some information is required such as the "Industry Sector" and "Material Type". Each material has a certain number. It can be external or internal according to the numbering system in the configuration. The data of each material are divided in the form of views. Simply views can be considered as multiple tabs, where each tab has a name and it contains information about the material. The name of the view is descriptive enough to be able to know the type of data exists within the view. The t-code used to access the material master data is "MM01".

Let's discuss the Material Types:

Material type is a way of definition for the criteria of the material. It can be used to differentiate between materials through the supply chain process. It is an identifier with four characters. We can have some examples of commonly used material types. 

Material Types can be Finished Products (FERT). It is type of products produced in-house, as it cannot be ordered by purchasing. Based on that it has no purchasing view in the material master record. Another material type can be Raw Materials (ROH). It is type of material that is procured externally then used according to the need of the organization. It contain purchasing view, but it doesn't contain sales as it cannot be sold. Another material type Semi- finished Products (HALB). It is type of material can be procured externally, and manufactured in-house. It can contain normally the purchasing view not as the Finished Products material type, as it can be procured externally.

Let's discuss the Pricing Conditions:

They are elements that contain the pricing and discounts information about a material from the vendor. It is important for the purchasing department to know the price of the material to get it at the appropriate price. Cost of the material contain components such as the gross price, discounts, taxes and shipment costs. That cost impacts the net and effective price. Conditions help to identify the cost components, and calculate the net or effective price.

Conditions can be time dependent and non time dependent conditions. Time dependent conditions have a defined period of time, which it is duration of applicability of the condition. Time dependent conditions can be used of purchase documents such as (PO, PR, etc.), while non time dependent conditions can be used in the info records. Info records are used to link a material to a vendor and price. It can be at the plant or purchasing organization level.

Let's discuss the Account Assignment Category and Item Category:

Account Assignment Category is used to categorize different needs of materials, and services. Different kinds of account category are needed for cost objects such as assets, production orders and cost centers. It can differs according to the requirements of Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt. Let's have some examples for more clarification. Regarding the assets, the main asset number, and sub numbers are required. Regarding the cost center, the cost center number and G/L account number are required for account assignment.

Item category identifies the way of procurement for the material or the service. Some examples of item categories, It can be standard, consignment, subcontracting, stock transport order, and third party type.

Let's discuss the Inventory Management in SAP MM:

Inventory management is used to store, and issue the goods. Goods have shelf life time, which is the expiry date of the goods. It is important to put them in batches, so all goods with the same expiry date should be kept together in one place. There are many activities included in the inventory management such as Managing Goods Receipts (GRs), Goods Issue (GI), physical inventory, Goods Return Management, Batch Management, Material Valuation, and Stock Transfer. 

There are three different types of material statuses. The Unrestricted-use stock, It is where materials can be used and transferred normally. The Inspection Stock, It is where materials are inspected to check it's quality. The Blocked Stock, It is where materials can be transferred to inspection or unrestricted use.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Usage Analytics Dashboard
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We have released a new feature that enables you to check your usage of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing option for Brazil by giving more accurate information.

The Usage Analytics dashboard can be accessed inside the SAP BTP Cockpit on the left navigation panel.

You can find all the usages within a period and its corresponding total.

There are two main groups to consider:

  • Group A: Documents Processed (for example NF-e processed, documents resent, XML files displayed, and e-mails resent)
  • Group B: SEFAZ tax authority environment (tpAmb tag), considering all Documents Processed for production and homologation

The sum of each group must be equal, which is represented by the label “Total documents from group A or B”.

Example:

Note in the following image that all usages with the suffix A sum up to 137, as indicated in the Total documents from group A or B metric. In the same way, the usages with B sum up to 137, of which there is only Homologation processed documents (B) since no Document Processed occurred for productive documents.

 

Main options of Usage Analytics

  • Capability: In the Usage Analytics dashboard, you can use the Capability drop-down to filter the usage of the service by selecting the capabilities of the SAP BTP (application, automation, or integration).
  • Service: In the Service drop-down, you can use it to filter the usage of each service plan in your company's subaccount. For example, you can filter the usage of data from the inbound or outbound invoicing option for Brazil.
  • Metric: You can select one of the metrics (documents that were displayed, downloaded, resent for approval, or processed). You can use the Metric drop-down to avoid showing all documents at the same time.
  • Period: Regarding the time range selected in the Period field, you should be aware that the information presented is only from 2024 onwards and only after installing the following SAP Notes.

Installation

You can implement these changes by following the instructions on the SAP Notes:

  • Outbound: SAP Note 3111469
  • Inbound: SAP Note 3399269

License Utilization Information (LUI)

The License Utilization Information (LUI) application provides an overview of license entitlement and software usage. To receive the results, a system measurement is performed on each relevant system.

The Usage Analytics dashboard can be used together with LUI by selecting the same period to generate insights about how the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance solution is being used. The LUI dashboard is expected to show the same value in column “Measured” as in the “Total document from group A or B” from Usage Analytics.

The LUI application can be accessed through SAP for Me. Check License Utilization Information for details on how to use and access it.

Date of accounting

The date of the metric may not represent the date of the action of the user. For example, there may be discrepancies where a document processed at the end of a month appears in the consumption of the following month, due to the asynchronous process of accounting. The timing of when a user takes an action and when that action is reflected in our metrics may not always align perfectly, especially around the transition between days.

You can contact your account executive if you have questions regarding the values or if you need further assistance. Check SAP Note 2626344.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “Like”, share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions in the SAP Community of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, and read similar blog posts in the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance blog space.

Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Bruno Eltz

entry.title: Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Release
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402 in the area of Professional Services. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests: Custom Roles and Multiple Recipients (4E9) 
  • Editability of Billing Due Dates in the Manage Project Billing Application (4E9) 
  • Improvements for On-Account Billing in Project Billing (4E9) 
  • Creation of Preliminary Billing Documents for Down Payment Requests (J14) 
  • Giving project managers direct access to stage change, billing document, staffing and time-recording information (J11) 
  • Enhanced WIP reporting for customer projects in case T&E billing (1IL)
  • Drag and drop assignment in Manage Resource Utilization app (BTP)

 

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Let’s get started with all the new topics in the project billing functionality. But before we do, remember that Andreas Hammerschmidt updates his detailed blog “Project Billing for Customer Projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud” with every release, so you can find a comprehensive overview of the functionality there.

 

Manage Workflow for Project Billing Requests: Custom Roles and Multiple Recipients (4E9)

When looking at the workflows we have in the billing process in the professional services scenario, it is always good to start at the overall process flow:

There you can see that we have workflow functionality in two places, one for the PBR (Project Billing Request) and one for the PBD (Preliminary Billing Document). The one which we are looking at today is the on the PBR side.

Now with this new release you are able to create custom roles for the workflow and also add in multiple recipients. You start off in the “Manage Workflow for Project Billing Request” app, which is off course where you create your workflow process. There you can see these additional roles. These are being created in the “Custom Logic” app. There you can implement a Badi as an administrator, to specify who are the workflow recipients.

If you are interested in the details on how to create that, please take a look at this video, where we explain the concrete steps:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Editability of Billing Due Dates in the Manage Project Billing Application (4E9)

With this next new functionality, we want to increase the flexibility in the billing process and speed up the bill preparation significantly.

In order to achieve this, now existing billing due dates in the Manage Project Billing application can be edited using the Planned Billing Dates dialog box. The billing specialist or billing team member can click on the billing due date of a billing element to open the dialog that lists the open billing plan items that belong to the billing element and edit their dates accordingly. 

With this feature, you can change the billing due dates of the billing elements of the following types:

  • Time and Expenses
  • Fixed Price
  • Periodic Services
  • Usage-Based

You must activate the feature POSTPONE_BILLING_DATE in order to use this new functionality. For more information on activating features, see Activate Features.

For this new functionality you can also have look on the details in this video:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Improvements for On-Account Billing in Project Billing (4E9)

In the 2402 release for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, we are adding functions such as selecting, merging and submitting of items in the on-account billing functionality. This will significantly increase the flexibility for the project billing specialist or billing team members during the preparation of an on-account bill.

So what exactly does this entail?

Let’s look at the new merging functionality, here we mean you can now merge open on account billing plan items that are yet to be billed with other contract types such as time and expense or fixed price.

For the selecting and submitting functionality, you can do so with open on-account billing plan items that have billing due dates in the past or future​.

And in addition, you will be able to edit billing due dates and amounts.

You want more? Take a look at this demo:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Creation of Preliminary Billing Documents for Down Payment Requests (J14)

With this next new functionality, we also see great value added to the overall process. Mainly we want to improve flexibility during the bill preparation process, facilitate handling of down payment requests in the billing process, and help ensure accuracy of bill preparation through the approval process for preliminary billing documents.

In short, Preliminary Billing Documents are now enabled for down payment requests. With this feature, you can create preliminary down payment requests to get down payment request details verified and approved by the involved stakeholders, before creating the actual down payment requests that request payments from customers. This verification and approval process improves the accuracy of the subsequent down payment requests and helps avoid the reworking of billing documents due to inaccurate billing values, for example, due to inaccurate down payment amounts. Preliminary down payment requests are optional. You can choose to use preliminary down payment requests for the verification and approval purpose, or you can skip this part of the process by creating down payment requests directly.

You can find a demo on this here:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Giving project managers direct access to stage change, billing document, staffing and time-recording information (J11)

Since the last release in August of 2023, there have been several new capabilities that have been added in this app. This makes the “Project Control – Professional Services Projects” app more and more the one-stop-shop for project managers as they will not need to collect professional services project related information from different places but can find them in one place and navigate from there to more detailed apps.

Now you might ask, what exactly are these new capabilities.

Well, here is a list. Users can now:

  • Understand who changed the project stage​
  • Open PDF preview for billing documents related to your project directly​
  • Get insights to the staffing situation for your projects, for example to find missing staffing or understand the cost impact of a certain staffing​

Get insights to the time recording situation for your project, for example to get an overview for missing recordings, over-booking or open approvals.

Have a look at the functionality in the system:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Enhanced WIP reporting for customer projects in case T&E billing (1IL)

 

The WIP on customer projects with T&E billing method needs to be reported and justified to internal and external stakeholders such as auditors. This must be possible for previous periods as well, thus we enhanced the journal entries of Event Based Revenue Recognition with additional information.

  • The Event Based Revenue Recognition journal entries have been enhanced by the hours and the billed product
  • write-off and CAP postings can now be determined by journal entry item fields
  • To enhance transparency we reduce the journal entry items for the CAP postings

 

To summarize, the WIP can now be explained by billed product and hours also for previous periods, and write-offs and cap postings can be analyzed in more detail. This will overall bring more information and therefore more transparency to the users.

Here is a demo:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Drag and drop assignment in Manage Resource Utilization app (BTP)

Let’s take a step away from the core functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and look closer at the Resource Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Projects, which is on the Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Resource managers can now by drag and drop just move an assignment from one person to another person. To handle changes in staffing is part of the daily tasks of resource manager. People are not any longer available and an assignment has to be moved to another candidate.

You can see a demo in the “Manage Resource Utilization” app here:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-excl... 
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Find out how to switch from groups to spaces and pages here: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/11/06/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cl...
  • Become an early adopter for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3612
  • Check out the SAP Activate Roadmap for early adopters here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapOvervie...
  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3

entry.title: Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part1
entry.content:
After customer/vendor contact person get synchronize to Business Partner, customer always get confused about where to find/maintain the corresponding address/communication field at BP side. Customer Master and Contact Person is using BAS (Business Address Service) for many years by using central address tables. Business Partner is using BAS as well with own address data sets. This makes whole address management complex. With this blog post I would like to bring some light into this topic.

3 types of KNVK address

Workplace address: Mandatory for KNVK record

Home address: Personnel or Private address of the contact person

Business address: alternative workplace address of the contact person address

Mapping between BP and customer/vendor contact person

Next, I will show mapping between BP and customer/vendor contact person. The following systems will be used:

E18_002

Workplace address

It is Mandatory for KNVK record

Sample data:

Customer 128 has contact person 0000023342.

I maintain telephone and e-mail address in below fields:

After CVI, customer 128 synchronize to BP 396.

Contact person 23342 synchronize to BP 395.

I can find the telephone and email address get synchronized here:

Click on Addr. Overview button, we can get below result.

How can we check at BAS table:

BUT020-ADDRNUMBER of BP-Org 396 = 43587

BUT000-PERSNUMBER of BP Person 395 = 43586

KNVK-PRSNR = 43584

KNA1-ADRNR = 43583

Related KBA about workplace address:

3348146 How the standard is designed related to contact person synchronization to BP

2516529 Communication data of contact person is not shown in BP relationships 

Please go ehead refer to below blog, since I seperate this article into three part

Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part 2

Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part 3

entry.title: Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part 2
entry.content:
3 types of KNVK address

Workplace address: Mandatory for KNVK record

Home address: Personnel or Private address of the contact person

Business address: alternative workplace address of the contact person address

Home address

Personnel or Private address of the contact person

Sample data: Customer 129 has contact person 23343

I maintain home address for contact person using another City. Also I maintain telephone number and email address.

After CVI, customer 129 get synchronized to Business Partner 398.

Contact person 23343 get synchronized to BP 397, we can find the telephone number and email address under this BP. The city and street information also shows here.

On relationship screen, it shows in below way:

Click on Addr. Overview button, we can get below result.

How can we check at BAS table:

BUT000-PERSNUMBER for BP person 397 = 43592

BUT020-ADDRNUMBER for BP person 397 = 0000043598

KNVK-ADRNP_2 = 43595

KNVK-PRSNR = 43590

Related KBA about home address:
3336713 Home Address for Contact Person 

See also:

Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part1

Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part 3

entry.title: Mapping of address data between Business Partner and customer/vendor contact person - Part 3
entry.content:
3 types of KNVK address

Workplace address: Mandatory for KNVK record

Home address: Personnel or Private address of the contact person

Business address: alternative workplace address of the contact person address

Business address

Business address: alternative workplace address of the contact person address

Sample data

Customer 130 has contact person 23344

I maintain business address for contact person using another City. Also I maintain telephone number and email address.

After CVI, customer get synchronized to BP 400.

BP 400 will then have two addresses. One gets synchronized from customer address, the other one gets synchronized from contact person business address. Here the standard address is the one which get synchronized from customer address.

Contact person 23344 get synchronized to BP 399.

On relationship screen, it shows in below way:

Click on the addr. Overview button, there are also two addresses. Here the standard address is the one which get synchronized from contact person’s business address.

How can we check at BAS table:

BUT052-ADDRNUMBER = 43606

BUT052-XDFADR = X.

BUT000-PERSNUMBER of BP Person = 43604

KNVK-ADRND = 43603

Related KBA about Business address:

3336683 

Business Address for Contact Person 

3353672 

How to find the business address assignment of a contact business partner  

3364586 

Business Address of Customer Contact Person not appearing in BP Screen after CVI. 

2914803 

How to map business address from BP Contact to Customer Contact 

2474655   

BUT052 entry is missing 

3154643 

Contact person address is not displayed on Partner tab in the Sales Order 

2842279 

Contact email does not appear in the collections worklist 

2519896 

Business Address Services tables are not updated correctly via BP transaction code 

3204162

Communication data is not fully copied to BP relationship.

entry.title: FAQ: Linking vendor and customer to same Business Partner
entry.content:
Proceed to the resolution section of this FAQ -Frequently Asked Questions- article for the answers of the following:

  1. When a customer is also a vendor, is it mandatory to synch them in only one BP?
  2. Prerequisites to synchronize customer and vendor to only one BP
  3. New transaction (CVI_LEDH) to check shared entities once customer and supplier master shall be assigned to same business partner

1.When a customer is also a vendor, is it mandatory to synch them in only one BP?

SAP recommends creating one BP per each legal entity and adding Roles for the different business functions used to interact with the company. However, it is not mandatory.

2.Prerequisites to synchronize customer and vendor to only one BP

SAP Note 2968536 must be implemented. Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2773288 - Customer and Vendor should link to same single Business Partner during initial load

If prerequisites are not meet, then below error will occur

            Consistency error:

            Message no. R1367

To solve the error, please refer to KBA: 2716945 - CVI: Consistency error - Message no. R1367

In the meanwhile, the error message (R1367) has been replaced and extended with respective context and explanation. Please consider implementing below notes:

3088419 - DDIC for Legal Entity BAdI Message (367) Enhancements  

3067086 - Legal Entity BAdI Message (367) Enhancements  

The message will be enhanced as below:

3.New transaction (CVI_LEDH) to check shared entities once customer and supplier master shall be assigned to same business partner

Please make sure below note are implemented.

 3072319 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report

 3088771 - DDIC Changes for Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report

 3082146 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report integration in CVI_COCKPIT 

 3081338 - Legal Entity - Data Harmonization (CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION) Report integration into MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT

entry.title: FAQ: BP category
entry.content:
Proceed to the resolution section of this FAQ -Frequently Asked Questions- article for the answers of the following:

1. BP category determination when creating a BP from a customer / vendor

2. How to activate CVI_MAP_TITLE_DIRECT or CVI_MAP_BP_CATEGORY

3.CVI fails with error R1231: "Form of address &1 not designated for organizations"

4.BP TYPE (BUT000-TYPE) cannot be changed after BP creation

1. BP category determination a BP is created from a customer / vendor.

For customers/vendors, there is no special attribute for organization, person and group while there is for business partners. When creating a BP from a customer / vendor, there is no information which type of BP shall be created. The only source is the title （LFA1-ANRED) and underlying customizing table TSAD3.（t-code SA03）

Per default the implementation CVI_MAP_TITLE_DIRECT of Badi CVI_MAP_TITLE is active ,which does not care about the business partner type and always leads to the creation of an organization.

When the implementation CVI_MAP_BP_CATEGORY of Badi CVI_MAP_TITLE is active, the title(LFA1-ANRED) of vendor or customer is considered to create the BP type accordingly.

2.How to active CVI_MAP_TITLE_DIRECT or CVI_MAP_BP_CATEGORY

Transaction SPRO :   Cross-Application Components

                              >  Master Data Synchronization

                                >    Customer/Vendor Integration

                                  >       Business Partner Settings

                                    >       Business Add-Ins (BAdIs)

                                      >        BAdI: Data Assignment of Form of Address from Customer/Vendor

Please note that BAdI CVI_MAP_TITLE allows only one active implementation, so if you want to activate BAdi CVI_MAP_TITLE_DIRECT , you need to de-activate BAdi CVI_MAP_BP_CATEGORY.

3.CVI fails with error R1231: "Form of address &1 not designated for organizations"

Detailed information could be found in KBA:  2000099 - CVI fails with error R1231: "Form of address &1 not designated for organizations"

4.BP TYPE (BUT000-TYPE) cannot be changed after creation

Detailed information could be found in KBA:

2585965 - Changing the business partner type from Organisation to person in transaction BP

entry.title: FAQ : Business Partner Tax
entry.content:
Proceed to the resolution section of this FAQ -Frequently Asked Questions- article for the answers of the following:

  1. Tax number categories that SAP delivers
  2. Customer needs to use a tax category for a country that is not included in note 775919
  3. Custom Tax number categories changed/deleted after system upgrade
  4. Mapping between Business Partner tax categories and ECC vendor/customer tax numbers
  5. Mapping between Table DFKKBPTAXNUM VAT number and table  KNAS/LFAS-STCEG, KNA1/LFA1-STCEG
  6. Error AR103: "<XXX> is longer than <XXX> characters"
  7. How to control tax duplicate check

1.Tax number categories that SAP delivers

Detailed information could be found in Note:775919 - Missing tax number categories of business partner

2.Customer needs to use a tax category for a country that is not included in note 775919

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2021660 - Missing tax number categories in note 775919

Note :  201073 - Adding more tax number types to the business partner

Note: Consider that this is a modification, that is, if SAP delivers a tax number type with the same name, the system in this case overwrites your modified tax number types according to the above-listed rules.

3.Custom Tax number categories changed/deleted after system upgrade

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2828534 - Tax number categories changed/deleted after upgrade.

4.Mapping between Business Partner tax categories and ECC vendor/customer tax numbers

The mapping between business partner tax categories and ECC vendor/customer tax numbers is as follows:

Tax category ending in 0 --- Goes to field tax “VAT Reg. No” (STCEG)
Tax category ending in 1 --- Goes to field “Tax Number 1” (STCD1)
Tax category ending in 2 --- Goes to field “Tax Number 2” (STCD2)
Tax category ending in 3 --- Goes to field “Tax Number 3” (STCD3)
Tax category ending in 4 --- Goes to field “Tax Number 4” (STCD4)
Tax category ending in 5 --- Goes to field “Tax Number 5” (STCD5)

The exceptions to the previous mapping rules are:

Country Germany(DE), Country China(CN).

Other special cases :

Argentina, Switzerland

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2865204 - Mapping Business Partner Tax Number Categories to customer/vendor Tax Number fields

5.Mapping between Table DFKKBPTAXNUM VAT number and table  KNAS/LFAS-STCEG, KNA1/LFA1-STCEG

If Address and Tax Country are the same then field KNA1-STCEG is used

If Address and Tax Country are different then field KNAS-STCEG is used

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2668405 - Table DFKKBPTAXNUM shows 2 VAT entries while table KNAS-STCEG only shows 1 VAT entry

6.Error AR103: "<XXX> is longer than <XXX> characters"

To solve this error, you can refer to KBA: 2477732 - Error AR103 happens when you add BP tax numbers

To change the error AR103 to warning message or info message, you can refer to Note: 958424 - BP:Error message for tax code 2 customized

7.How to control tax duplicate check

Please use transaction BUPA_TAXNUMTYPE which gives the tax categories for the countries.

If duplicate check is off then no duplicate check will be done for the vat number.

If you put the duplicate check on then you will you will get BUPA_TAX 400 which is a message which checks for duplicate tax entries.

entry.title: FAQ: BP/Customer/Vendor change log
entry.content:
Proceed to the resolution section of this FAQ -Frequently Asked Questions- article for the answers of the following:

  1. How to check table CDHDR, CDPOS
  2. Objects are saved with different time zone in the database tables like CDHDR
  3. Useful t-code to check change log
  4. Changes made to some fields are not reported in the change history
  5. Performance issue when you want to see change history of Business Partner

1.How to check table CDHDR, CDPOS

2.Objects are saved with different time zone in the database tables like CDHDR

Each object saves the change document in the database in different time zone.

From BP side, all BUPA* objects will save the data on UTC time.

For other objects like ADRESSE, ADRESSE2,ADRESSE3, ISMBP,DEBI,KRED the data is saved on user time zone.

Detailed information could be found in KBA:  1596509 - Change documents for Business Partners have different date and time in database

KBA: 2033550 - CAA2 / BP / FPP2 / INSOCHANGE / PSOBWORK: change time incorrect displayed in change history

3.Useful t-code to check change log

T-code S_ALR_87012089 is used to check change log of Vendor
T-code S_ALR_87012182 (OV51) is  used to check change log of Customer
T-code BPCD (report BUSCHDOC)is used to check change log of Business Partner

Transaction OV51 demonstrates more changes than within transactions XD02/XD03

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 1973615 - OV51 shows more changes than tx. XD02/XD03

The Change History in the environment of Master Data Objects only shows Change Documents which are important.

For a complete overview of Change Documents of a master data object the report BUSCHDOC can be used
Detailed information could be found in KBA:
2403347 - Change History of Master Data Objects in FI-CA is incomplete / shows not all Change Documents

4.Changes made to some fields are not reported in the change history

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2461930 - Custom fields change history not captured

KBA: 2434836 - Customer / Vendor Change Log missing query

5.Performance issue when you want to see change history of Business Partner

Detailed information could be found in KBA: 2126752 - Performance Problems when reading Business Partner Change Documents

entry.title: Generally available now: SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
entry.content:
I’m very happy to share that  SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition (the successor of Process Discovery and Pathfinder on Spotlight) is now  generally available! 🚀

You can learn more about this offering here.

What will happen with Process Discovery, Pathfinder on Spotlight and Spotlight?

Starting today (15th February), the only offering available to you is SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition  and it is NOT possible to request Process Discovery, Pathfinder and Spotlight anymore.
Process Discovery, Pathfinder on Spotlight, and Spotlight will be completely sunset on March 31st.

How to order the new offering?

To get your free analysis, you just need to do a data extraction from your SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system (you will need to implement the latest version of the SAP Notes  2758146 and 2745851) and request SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition via this page: SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition request page.

Out now: Access the PDF Executive Summary

At the moment the PDF Executive Summary is on request, but do not hesitate to request it (for analyses already delivered) by contacting Signavio-Disocvery@sap.com. We are very much looking for your feedback.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2024-1 for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has started with the first cycle of the 2024 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI). The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

We are offering several interesting opportunities for customers to influence SAP’s S/4HANA solutions.

Find here the list of all projects that are relevant for the SAP S4/HANA Community or browse the whole project list here.

The projects will be open for customer registration until March 08, 2024.

SAP S/4HANA projects for registration:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

  • GenAI for Situation Handling 
  • Integration of SAP Financial Compliance Management with SAP Identity Access Governance 
  • Intelligent Closing in the Public Cloud ERP 
  • Leveraging Generative AI capabilities in Business Partner Master data 
  • Planned New Cost Center Reporting with Review Booklets in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Qualitative risk assessment methodology in SAP Financial Compliance Management 

 

Supply Chain Management 

  • Advanced Planning and Scheduling (PP/DS) in SAP S/4HANA 
  • Business AI in Asset Performance Management 
  • Planned SAP BTP Service for Dynamic Forms 
  • SAP PLM - Change Management for SAP EPD 
  • SAP Service and Asset Management - Safety Technician Persona(WCM) 

How do I register on the Customer Influence website? A short overview about how to find and register for a CEI project can be found on influence.sap.com.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation!
